_doc: Sample BM25 search hits — fed into search.html via render_template('search.html', results=...). Each item maps to a Result row in _result_item.html. Dates/URLs are example homelab services; replace at port-time.

results.title: Defining alerting rules — Prometheus
results.snippet_html: Alerting rules allow you to define <mark>alert</mark> conditions based on Prometheus expression language expressions and to send notifications about firing alerts to an external service. Whenever the <mark>alert</mark> expression results in one or more vector elements at a given point in time, the <mark>alert</mark> counts as active for these elements' label sets.

results.title: Grafana — Prometheus alerts dashboard
results.snippet_html: Live view of all firing and pending Prometheus <mark>alerts</mark> across the homelab. Includes group-by-severity panels and a 24h history.

results.title: Recording rules vs. alerting rules
results.snippet_html: Recording rules let you precompute frequently needed or computationally expensive expressions and save their result as a new set of time series. <mark>Alerting</mark> rules act on those series to fire notifications.

vector_hits.title: Why your alert flapped at 3am
vector_hits.snippet: Postmortem covering for: clause sensitivity and how Alertmanager group_wait interacts with PagerDuty escalation.

vector_hits.title: Alertmanager silences API
vector_hits.snippet: REST endpoint for creating and querying silences programmatically. Useful for maintenance windows.

vector_hits.title: Notification routing tree
vector_hits.snippet: Decision diagram for which receiver fires for a given alert label set.

ai_summary.html: Prometheus alerting rules live in *.rules.yml files referenced by rule_files: in prometheus.yml. They evaluate PromQL expressions on every scrape interval; matching elements transition through pending → firing based on the for: clause, then Alertmanager groups, dedupes and routes them to receivers.
